g.text: Standalone process → read source events → normalize to ingest.v1 → submit to Switchboard via MCP → checkpoint → repeat
No direct DB access to butler schemas | No message classification | No request_id minting
C. ingest.v1 ENVELOPE — EXPLODED VIEW (5 FIELDS)
ingest.v1 envelope { source, event_type, sender_ref, content, metadata }
source = channel identifier | event_type = message|reaction|edit|delete|status | metadata = channel-specific extras (thread_id, message_id, ...)
IngestAcceptedResponse → request_id (UUID7) | status: "accepted" | duplicate: bool | triage_decision | triage_target
Connector-level dedup is best-effort | Switchboard idempotency key is authoritative | Switchboard returns duplicate=true when already accepted
E. CRASH-SAFE CHECKPOINTS F. RATE LIMITING (DUAL LIMITER)
Write cursor to DB via cursor_store
CRASH: restart reads last cursor → replay is
Independent limiters — source API failure does NOT block Switchboard submissions

All connectors share: CachedMCPClient (SSE transport) | ConnectorMetrics (Prometheus) | Health server (/health + /metrics) | Heartbeat task | FilteredEventBuffer | Checkpoint-after-acceptance semantics
Applied in order: Layer 1 (label filter) gates ingestion before tier assignment. Layers 2 and 3 run only if label check passes.
Runs FIRST — gates ingestion before tiering
